query:
Research the company "anheuser busch".

Research the company to identify: (1) Recent CEO or C-suite transitions (within last 12 months); (2) Latest earnings reports or earnings misses—specifically P&L gaps, margin compression, or revenue shortfalls despite functional wins; (3) Recent guidance changes, especially pulled or revised 2026 guidance; (4) Evidence of multiple growth drivers operating semi-independently (media/marketing spend, production capacity, pricing strategy, distribution network); (5) Recent platform launches, brand expansions, or portfolio changes; (6) Company scale (revenue, market cap) and industry; (7) Any public signals of strategic reviews or restructuring initiatives. Focus on companies in industries where cross-functional optimization (production, pricing, distribution, marketing) directly impacts P&L outcomes (e.g., consumer goods, automotive, CPG, specialty manufacturing).

Focus on the last 6 months. Return a comprehensive summary with specific facts, numbers, and dates.
answer:
**Executive Summary**
Anheuser-Busch InBev (AB InBev) generated $59.3 billion in FY 2025 revenue, operating as a massive scale player in the global consumer beverage industry. Over the last six months, the company has navigated regional revenue shortfalls and slight margin compression by leveraging semi-independent growth drivers, including aggressive premiumization pricing, digital marketing overhauls, and the expansion of its B2B/D2C distribution platforms. The company is currently undergoing significant leadership restructuring, highlighted by the transition of its long-time CFO and U.S. CMO, while maintaining its 2026 guidance of 4-8% organic EBITDA growth.

---

### 1. Financial Performance: P&L Gaps and Margin Compression
AB InBev's recent earnings reveal a mixed P&L landscape, characterized by top-line growth offset by regional headwinds and margin pressures. 

*   **Revenue Shortfalls & Earnings Misses:** In Q3 2025, AB InBev experienced a revenue miss of 0.90% ($12.71 billion actual vs. $12.82 billion expected) and a significant earnings miss of 31.43% ($0.45 actual vs. $0.65 expected) [GuruFocus](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/8604887/anheuserbusch-inbev-sanv-xbruabi-q4-2025-earnings-report-preview-what-to-expect). For Q4 2025, reports were mixed: while one source noted revenue reached $15.55 billion to beat expectations [Value The Markets](https://www.valuethemarkets.com/news/anheuser-busch-inbev-beats-q4-2025-earnings-estimates), another reported that Q4 revenues missed the Zacks Consensus Estimate [Nasdaq](https://www.nasdaq.com/articles/ab-inbev-q4-earnings-beat-business-momentum-revenues-miss). FY 2025 total revenue slightly declined to $59.3 billion from $59.8 billion in FY 2024 [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260504526109/en/AB-InBev-Reports-First-Quarter-2026-Results).
*   **Margin Compression:** In Q4 2025, normalized EBITDA margin contracted by 10 basis points year-over-year to 35.2% on both a reported and organic basis [The Globe and Mail](https://www.theglobeandmail.com/investing/markets/stocks/CABGY/pressreleases/188609/ab-inbev-q4-earnings-beat-on-business-momentum-revenues-miss/). In Q1 2026, EBITDA margins remained "flattish," requiring disciplined resource allocation to offset transactional foreign exchange (FX) headwinds [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260504526109/en/AB-InBev-Reports-First-Quarter-2026-Results). 
*   **Regional Headwinds:** The company faced a notable Q4 2025 revenue decline in China due to volume headwinds [TipRanks](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/bud/earnings). In Q1 2026, South Korean volumes declined by low-teens and revenue fell by high-single digits due to shipment phasing following an April 2025 price increase [Business Wire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260504526109/en/AB-InBev-Reports-First-Quarter-2026-Results). Furthermore, 2026 Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) headwinds are heavily weighted toward the first half of the year, specifically in Mexico and Brazil [TIKR](https://www.tikr.com/blog/anheuser-busch-inbev-call-record-eps-and-12-revenue-growth).
*   **Q1 2026 Rebound:** Despite earlier misses, Q1 2026 revenue reached $15.267 billion, a 12.0% reported and 5.8% organic year-over-year increase [Tickeron](https://tickeron.com/blogs/anheuser-busch-inbev-bud-q1-2026-earnings-beat-highlights-premiumization-strength-13148/).

### 2. 2026 Guidance and Capital Allocation
There is no evidence that AB InBev has pulled or downwardly revised its 2026 guidance. 
*   **2026 Outlook:** Management has guided for full-year 2026 organic EBITDA growth of 4-8%, net capital expenditures of $3.5-$4.0 billion, and a normalized effective tax rate of 26-28% [Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/anheuser-busch-inbev-sa-nv-200852549.html). Analysts note this mid-single-digit growth aligns with the company's existing medium-term outlook [Maintained Buy on Anheuser-Busch InBev: Attractive Upside Persists Despite Lowered Price Target and Modest Estimate Cuts - TipRanks.com](https://www.tipranks.com/news/ratings/maintained-buy-on-anheuser-busch-inbev-attractive-upside-persists-despite-lowered-price-target-and-modest-estimate-cuts-ratings-news).
*   **Share Buybacks:** In October 2025, the company launched a $6 billion share buyback program. As of February 9, 2026, approximately $635 million in shares had been repurchased [Finviz](https://finviz.com/news/309573/ab-inbev-q4-earnings-beat-on-business-momentum-revenues-miss).
*   **Debt:** AB InBev finished 2025 with a leverage ratio of 2.87x, absorbing a $2.8 billion FX headwind on net debt [TipRanks](https://www.tipranks.com/stocks/bud/earnings).

### 3. C-Suite and Leadership Transitions
AB InBev is executing a significant leadership overhaul centered around its April 29, 2026, Annual General Meeting:
*   **CFO Transition:** Longtime CFO Felipe Dutra is stepping down after the April 2026 shareholder meeting. He is being replaced by Fernando Tennenbaum, a 15-year company veteran who previously served as VP of the South America division and CFO of Ambev [CFO.com](https://www.cfo.com/news/anheuser-busch-inbev-cfo-to-step-down/656932/).
*   **Marketing Leadership:** U.S. Chief Marketing Officer Benoit Garbe resigned at the end of 2025. U.S. Chief Commercial Officer Kyle Norrington has assumed oversight of marketing duties [Marketing Dive](https://www.marketingdive.com/news/ab-inbev-cmo-resign-bud-light-boycott-backlash/699989/). Additionally, two marketing executives were placed on leave in February 2026 following a brand collaboration controversy [Food Dive](https://www.fooddive.com/news/ab-inbev-marketing-executives-leave-absence-controversy/648474/).
*   **Broader C-Suite Shuffle:** Nelson Jamel was promoted to Chief People Officer, and David Almeida transitioned to Chief Strategy and Technology Officer [Brewbound](https://www.brewbound.com/news/anheuser-busch-inbev-cfo-to-step-down-replacement-named/). Chair Martin Barrington is also scheduled to retire following the April 2026 meeting [The Globe and Mail](https://www.theglobeandmail.com/investing/markets/stocks/BUD/pressreleases/1225773/goldman-sachs-reaffirms-their-buy-rating-on-anheuser-busch-inbev-sa-bud/).

### 4. Cross-Functional Growth Drivers & Platform Expansions
AB InBev is utilizing semi-independent operational levers to drive growth despite regional volume pressures:

*   **Media & Marketing Spend:** The company invested $7.4 billion in sales and marketing in 2025 [Anheuser-Busch InBev SA/NV Earnings - Analysis & Highlights for Q4 2025](https://www.alpha-sense.com/earnings/bud/). By 2025, nearly 40% of this spend was reallocated to digital channels via its ALICE platform [What is Sales and Marketing Strategy of Anheuser-Busch InBev Company? – MatrixBCG.com](https://matrixbcg.com/blogs/marketing-strategy/ab-inbev), with over 60% of global media spend dedicated to social, programmatic, and influencer channels [What is Sales and Marketing Strategy of Anheuser-Busch InBev Company? – businessmodelcanvastemplate.com](https://businessmodelcanvastemplate.com/blogs/marketing-strategy/anheuser-busch-inbev-marketing-strategy). AI analytics have improved marketing effectiveness by approximately 15% over two fiscal years [What is Sales and Marketing Strategy of Anheuser-Busch InBev Company? – businessmodelcanvastemplate.com](https://businessmodelcanvastemplate.com/blogs/marketing-strategy/anheuser-busch-inbev-marketing-strategy).
*   **Pricing Strategy (Premiumization):** Revenue per hectoliter increased by 5.8% (reported) and 4.5% (organic) in Q1 2026, driven by revenue management and a positive mix from premiumization and "Beyond Beer" categories [Stocktitan](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/BUD/6-k-anheuser-busch-in-bev-sa-nv-current-report-foreign-issuer-7489e6e5566f.html). 
*   **Production Capacity & Restructuring:** The company is doubling its U.S. manufacturing investment to $600 million across 2025 and 2026 [Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/economy/policy/articles/anheuser-busch-doubles-u-manufacturing-165745662.html), including $5.8 million for its Williamsburg, VA brewery to expand Michelob ULTRA production [Ad-Hoc-News](https://www.ad-hoc-news.de/boerse/news/ueberblick/anheuser-busch-inbev-sa-nv-stock-be0974293251-fresh-us-investment-plan/69401964). Concurrently, AB InBev has closed some breweries to improve supply chain efficiency [Manufacturing Dive](https://www.manufacturingdive.com/news/anheuser-busch-doubles-us-manufacturing-investment/818292/) and implemented a "touchless planning" supply chain overhaul using o9 Solutions software [Consumer Goods Technology](https://consumergoods.com/why-ab-inbevs-supply-chain-overhaul-focused-touchless-planning). On January 30, 2026, the company re-acquired a 49.9% minority stake in its U.S. metal container plants from an Apollo Global Management-led consortium [Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/ab-inbev-reports-full-fourth-060100661.html).
*   **Distribution Networks & Platform Launches:** 
    *   **B2B:** The BEES digital platform reached over 3.7 million monthly active users across 20+ countries, capturing over $35 billion in gross merchandise value by early 2025 [What is Sales and Marketing Strategy of Anheuser-Busch InBev Company? – MatrixBCG.com](https://matrixbcg.com/blogs/marketing-strategy/ab-inbev).
    *   **D2C:** Zé Delivery in Brazil fulfills over 60 million orders annually, and total D2C orders across platforms like TaDa Delivery hit 70 million in 2024 [What is Sales and Marketing Strategy of Anheuser-Busch InBev Company? – MatrixBCG.com](https://matrixbcg.com/blogs/marketing-strategy/ab-inbev).
    *   **Infrastructure:** Over $2 billion has been invested in Route-to-Market (RTM) transformations in Africa and Southeast Asia [What is Sales and Marketing Strategy of Anheuser-Busch InBev Company? – businessmodelcanvastemplate.com](https://businessmodelcanvastemplate.com/blogs/marketing-strategy/anheuser-busch-inbev-marketing-strategy).

---

### Gaps and Limitations
Based strictly on the provided research findings, the following requested data points could not be fulfilled:
*   **Market Capitalization:** The current market cap of AB InBev was not included in the source data.
*   **Pulled Guidance:** There is no evidence in the provided text of AB InBev pulling or downwardly revising its 2026 guidance; findings only show reaffirmed guidance and analyst expectations of meeting/beating targets.
